FEAST Lab · Mizzou

Our Research

We are a transdisciplinary group of researchers working at the interface of science, engineering, and technology — addressing Hidden Hunger (micronutrient deficiency) through circular food systems innovation.

Why FEAST Research?

A world with enough food —
yet 2 billion go hungry

Our world, this beautiful blue planet produces enough food to nourish its entire population, yet nearly one-third is lost or wasted each year.

At the same time, over two billion people suffer from hidden hunger caused by micronutrient deficiencies, contributing to the global double burden of malnutrition.

The FEAST Research Group develops sustainable food systems innovations to address these interconnected challenges and advance both food and nutrition security.

~1/3
Food produced is lost or wasted each year
2B+
People suffering from hidden hunger globally

Building a sustainable and nutritious future for all through circular food systems.

The Problem We Solve
FEAST Lab — Vision
Building a sustainable and nutritious future for all through circular food systems.
Three Core Areas
Research Areas
Centered on UN SDG 2 — Zero Hunger
01
🧪
Food Fortification

Fortification of foods with multiple micronutrients to address hidden hunger (micronutrient deficiencies).

  • Double & triple fortification of salt with iron, iodine & folic acid
  • Quadruple fortification including Vitamin B12
  • Microencapsulation of ferrous fumarate for scale-up
  • Vitamin B12 encapsulation in soymilk powder
  • Beetroot processing for enhanced iron retention
02
⚗️
Innovative Technologies

Scaling-up strategies and designing nano-micro-macro scale encapsulation systems for micronutrient delivery.

  • Spray drying & microencapsulation optimization
  • Green nanotechnology — gold nanoparticles from grape waste
  • Beta-cyclodextrin molecular inclusion of catechin
  • Microwave-assisted extraction of phenolic antioxidants
  • In-vitro release profiling in food media
03
♻️
Sustainable Food Systems Engineering

Creating a circular economy by upcycling food loss/waste and recovering micronutrients from underutilized native plants.

  • Zero-waste soybean processing & by-product valorization
  • Upcycling Greek yogurt acid whey with millets
  • Elderberry, pawpaw & black walnut processing
  • Ancient grains: millets, sorghum & amaranth
  • Mango waste upcycling in East Africa

Aligned with UN Sustainable Development Goals

All FEAST Lab research is centered on SDG 2 — Zero Hunger, working toward a world where everyone has access to safe, nutritious, and sufficient food. Our work bridges cutting-edge food engineering with real-world impact in food and nutrition security across the USA, India, Canada, and Kenya.

External Funding

Grants & Sponsored Research

Dr. Krishnaswamy has secured competitive funding from NSF, USDA, VentureWell, and industry partners to advance food systems innovation.
